coreCompetencies: Understanding of the difference between coaching a team and facilitating its events
Basic knowledge of coaching stances such as teaching, mentoring and facilitating
Ability to observe a team and record patterns without intervening
Familiarity with the distinction between output metrics and outcome metrics
Understanding of why imposed process rarely produces lasting change
Ability to prepare materials for a training session on agile basics
Basic notions of active listening and powerful questions
Documentation of observations from team and management interactions
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with common agile frameworks and their vocabulary
Basic knowledge of facilitation techniques
Understanding of the role of a Scrum Master versus a coach
indicators: Supports a senior coach in engagements

coreCompetencies: Coaching of a single team beyond process, on collaboration and ownership
Delivery of training sessions on agile practices to teams and new hires
Diagnosis of whether a team's problem is process, skill or environment
Distinction between vanity metrics and metrics that reflect real outcomes
Facilitation of a team's improvement backlog toward measurable change
Coaching of a Scrum Master on facilitation and impediment removal
Observation of management behavior and its effect on the team
Support of a small change initiative within one team
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of team maturity and development models
Understanding of Kanban flow beyond a single framework
Familiarity with retrospective formats and facilitation patterns
indicators: Coaches one team beyond ceremony facilitation

coreCompetencies: Coaching of several teams and their leads across a delivery group
Diagnosis of organizational impediments that sit above any single team
Design of a training path that grows Scrum Masters within the organization
Coaching of first-line managers on delegating and enabling autonomy
Definition of outcome metrics that resist being gamed as targets
Facilitation of a change initiative spanning more than one team
Coaching of teams and managers on continuous improvement systems
Assessment of team health across a group to prioritize coaching effort
complementaryCompetencies: Knowledge of value stream mapping across teams
Understanding of change management fundamentals
Familiarity with systems thinking applied to delivery
indicators: Coaches multiple teams and their leads

coreCompetencies: Coaching of middle management on the behaviors that enable team autonomy
Organizational diagnosis that identifies structural causes of poor flow
Design of a continuous improvement system that outlives any single coach
Establishment of outcome metrics as the basis for judging change success
Facilitation of a multi-team transformation with clear success criteria
Coaching of Scrum Masters as a community of practice, not one by one
Management of resistance to change among managers and teams
Separation of genuine agility from process theater in an organization
complementaryCompetencies: Experience redesigning team topologies to reduce dependencies
Knowledge of incentive structures that shape behavior
Understanding of organizational culture assessment methods
indicators: Coaches middle management, not just teams
Leads a multi-team change with clear metrics

coreCompetencies: Design of an organization-wide transformation with staged, measurable outcomes
Coaching of senior managers on the leadership shifts a transformation requires
Diagnosis of the systemic causes of delivery problems across departments
Definition of the metrics leadership uses to tell real change from theater
Establishment of a coaching capability that the organization can sustain itself
Management of change across teams that report to different leaders
Facilitation of alignment between agility goals and business strategy
Development of Scrum Masters and coaches through structured growth paths
complementaryCompetencies: Experience with organizational design for flow efficiency
Knowledge of change models such as Kotter or ADKAR
Understanding of how budgeting cycles constrain agility

coreCompetencies: Leadership of agile transformation across a business unit or division
Coaching of executives on the operating-model changes that unlock delivery
Diagnosis of how structure, funding and incentives block or enable agility
Definition of the outcome metrics by which leadership steers the change
Design of the coaching operating model across many teams and managers
Management of large-scale change resistance at the leadership layer
Establishment of continuous improvement as an organizational habit
Exposure of process theater and redirection toward real business outcomes
complementaryCompetencies: Experience aligning transformation with organizational strategy
Knowledge of portfolio-level flow and funding models
Understanding of leadership development at scale

coreCompetencies: Direction of enterprise-wide agile transformation across the organization
Coaching of the executive team on the culture and structure change demands
Organizational diagnosis that reaches the operating and governance model
Definition of the enterprise metrics that separate real agility from ritual
Design of the transformation strategy with staged, verifiable outcomes
Management of resistance rooted in company structure and power dynamics
Establishment of a coaching organization that outlasts individual engagements
Alignment of the transformation with the company's strategic direction
complementaryCompetencies: Experience leading transformation across a large enterprise
Knowledge of how governance reshapes to support flow
Understanding of executive coaching at the top of an organization
indicators: Owns the transformation strategy and its metrics

coreCompetencies: Definition of the enterprise thesis on how the organization becomes and stays agile
Coaching of the executive team on the structural changes agility requires of them
Diagnosis of the deepest systemic constraints in the operating model
Establishment of the metrics that hold the whole organization to real outcomes
Design of transformations that change how the company funds and governs work
Building of a coaching discipline that the organization sustains without founders
Exposure and dismantling of process theater at organizational scale
Alignment of the change agenda with the company's long-term strategy
complementaryCompetencies: Advisory to executives on agile operating models across the industry
Influence on transformation practice beyond the company
Track record of transformations that held years after the engagement
indicators: Advises the executive team on structural change
Is a recognized authority in agile transformation

coreCompetencies: Ownership of the enterprise strategy for organizational agility and its evolution
Coaching of the most senior leaders on the behaviors that sustain change
Diagnosis and removal of the structural constraints embedded in the operating model
Definition of the outcome metrics that govern the organization's improvement
Direction of transformation as a permanent capability, not a one-off program
Establishment of a coaching organization that renews itself over time
Setting of the company's principled stance against ritual and theater
Alignment of organizational agility with long-term business strategy
complementaryCompetencies: Recognition as a leader in organizational agility across the industry
Influence on transformation and coaching practice beyond the company
Track record of building lasting coaching organizations at scale
indicators: Leads organizational agility for the whole enterprise
Is a reference authority in agile transformation
